source.title: Dynamics of Awake Hippocampal-Prefrontal Replay for Spatial Learning and Memory-Guided Decision Making

confidence_rationale: High-quality in vivo recording in same hippocampal-prefrontal ensembles throughout learning; directly shows the same population switches between reverse (retrospective evaluation) and forward (prospective planning) replay modes as a function of learning phase. Core claim maps closely, but REE uses E2_x forward model weights rather than place cell firing sequences. Transfer risk from rat spatial task to general REE architecture is moderate.
failure_signatures: If forward and reverse replay require distinct place cell subpopulations (not same cells), the single-substrate claim for MECH-257 is weakened.
If the learning shift is driven by context change rather than a controller signal, the gating mechanism in MECH-257 may not generalise.

mapping.source_claim_statement: During pauses between behavioural trajectories, reverse hippocampal replay supports retrospective evaluation of past paths and forward replay supports prospective planning of future paths; the same hippocampal-prefrontal ensembles carry both, with opposing learning gradients across a task session.
mapping.ree_translation: E2_x is a single substrate read in two modes: retrospective comparator (post-action residual) and prospective rollout-scoring (pre-action evaluation). The mode shift Shin et al. document across learning is a behavioural analogue of the controller-gated mode switch MECH-257 proposes, with hippocampal sharp-wave ripple direction (forward/reverse) as the biological gating signal.
mapping.mapping_caveat: REE posits a moment-by-moment controller gate (MECH-094 commitment boundary), whereas Shin et al. describe a session-level learning gradient; whether the same cell population supports sub-second mode flipping within a single episode is not tested. The paper also cannot distinguish whether the same neurons contribute to both modes or whether there is task-phase-specific recruitment.
mapping.source_context: Awake rat spatial alternation task, hippocampal CA1 + dorsal mPFC ensemble recordings, sharp-wave ripple-triggered replay analysis.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron 2019, Jadhav lab; single-unit + LFP, large ensembles, within-subject longitudinal. Mapping fidelity high but not perfect: REE claim is about weight-sharing across modes, not place-cell identity per se.
